report_name: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 Concludes with $1.3M Awarded for 47 Zero-Days in Enterprise Software

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used to achieve remote code execution with SYSTEM privileges on Microsoft Exchange Server via a three-bug chain, and also used against Microsoft SharePoint in a two-bug chain during the Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 competition.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: A sophisticated exploit against VMware ESXi was demonstrated, which included a cross-tenant code execution component.

vulnerabilities.name: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 Zero-Days
vulnerabilities.description: A set of 47 unique zero-day vulnerabilities disclosed at the Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 competition. These vulnerabilities were discovered in major enterprise software products and demonstrated via exploits during the event.

campaigns.name: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
campaigns.description: An ethical hacking competition held from May 14-16, 2026, where security researchers demonstrated exploits against enterprise software, resulting in the disclosure of 47 zero-day vulnerabilities.
campaigns.objective: Discover and demonstrate zero-day vulnerabilities in major enterprise software products for responsible disclosure.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor of multiple products targeted in the competition, including Exchange Server, SharePoint, Windows 11, and Edge, for which zero-day vulnerabilities were disclosed.

identities.name: VMware
identities.description: Vendor of VMware ESXi, which was successfully exploited with a cross-tenant code execution vulnerability during the Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 competition.

identities.name: Red Hat
identities.description: Vendor of Red Hat Enterprise Linux, which was a target of a successful exploit at the Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 competition.

identities.name: OpenAI
identities.description: Vendor of AI platforms, including OpenAI Codex, which were targeted in the Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 competition, highlighting AI as a new attack surface.
